title: tagetes imbricata
intro: Tagetes imbricata, also known as Mexican marigold or tetragonula, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family, Asteraceae, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Tagetes imbricata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Tagetes imbricata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and its flowers and leaves can also be used in traditional folk medicine.
